title: MiFID II Transaction Reporting Amendments
content:
INTERNAL COMPLIANCE MEMORANDUM

TO: Senior Advisors and Portfolio Managers (Tier 3+)
FROM: Regulatory Compliance Department
DATE: June 15, 2023
RE: Updated Transaction Reporting Requirements Under MiFID II Framework

This memorandum addresses critical amendments to transaction reporting obligations that directly impact our client portfolio management activities and regulatory submissions to competent authorities.

EFFECTIVE REPORTING THRESHOLDS

Per MiFIR Article 26 requirements, all equity transactions exceeding €15,000 in notional value must be reported within T+1 to the relevant National Competent Authority (NCA). For fixed income securities, the threshold remains at €100,000 notional or equivalent market value at execution. Our current AUM of €2.8 billion across 847 client accounts necessitates heightened attention to these reporting triggers.

DATA FIELD REQUIREMENTS UNDER RTS 25

ESMA RTS 25 mandates specific data elements for each reportable transaction. Key fields include:
- Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) for institutional clients above €500M AUM
- Investment decision maker identification using national client identifiers
- Execution venue classification (SI, MTF, OTF, or regulated market)
- Short selling indicator for equity positions
- Commodity derivative classification codes where applicable

For our private wealth clients, particular attention must be paid to the anonymization requirements when reporting transactions below the €10M portfolio threshold.

CLIENT CLASSIFICATION IMPACTS

Professional clients with portfolios exceeding €500,000 require enhanced transaction detail reporting, including execution quality metrics and best execution venue analysis. This affects approximately 312 of our current client relationships. Retail clients remain subject to standard reporting protocols unless individual transactions exceed €50,000 notional value.

SYSTEM INTEGRATION UPDATES

Our Bloomberg AIM and Charles River IMS platforms have been updated to automatically flag reportable transactions based on the amended thresholds. The compliance dashboard now includes real-time monitoring of:
- Daily reportable transaction volumes
- Failed submission alerts
- LEI validation status for corporate clients
- Missing execution venue data

REPORTING TIMELINE COMPLIANCE

All reportable transactions must be submitted by 23:59 CET on T+1. Late submissions incur regulatory penalties starting at €25,000 per incident for systematic failures. Our current compliance rate stands at 99.2%, with remaining failures primarily attributed to incomplete client identification data for cross-border transactions.

DELEGATION ARRANGEMENTS

For clients utilizing our discretionary portfolio management services (approximately 67% of total AUM), Meridian Wealth Partners maintains reporting responsibility. Third-party execution arrangements through our prime brokerage relationships require coordination with reporting delegates to ensure no duplication or omission of required submissions.

ACTION ITEMS FOR SENIOR ADVISORS

1. Verify LEI status for all institutional clients above €500M threshold
2. Update client classification documentation in CRM system
3. Review discretionary mandate agreements for reporting clause compliance
4. Ensure execution venue preferences align with reporting capabilities

Non-compliance with these requirements may result in regulatory sanctions, including temporary restrictions on client onboarding activities and mandatory remediation programs.

For technical questions regarding system implementations, contact the Trade Operations desk at extension 4721. Regulatory interpretation queries should be directed to the Compliance team at compliance@meridianwp.eu.

This memorandum supersedes the previous guidance dated March 2023 and remains effective until further regulatory updates are issued by ESMA or relevant NCAs.
